title: Keep package rollback scoped to changed target hosts
prompt: Repair the rollback behavior in `bin/package-rollout` without changing its public CLI or the protected tests. The rollout simulator supports label queries, dry runs, artifact checksum verification, bounded install and health-check phases, partial failures, and a rollback journal. A failed rollout currently performs rollback work on hosts that were never successfully changed, including hosts outside the target query. Rollback must use the successfully installed hosts recorded for the current rollout as its source of truth: restore every successfully changed target, but never write package state, an events.log rollback entry, or a rollback journal entry for an install-failed target or an untargeted host. Preserve the existing failure codes, journal format, dry-run behavior, and bounded-concurrency behavior. Do not modify `tests/run-tests.sh`. Verification succeeds with `bash tests/run-tests.sh` from the repository root.
